11.reason: [Using `gets()` is unsafe and deprecated; it was removed in C11 and causes compilation errors with modern compilers due to security risks.]

34.reason: [This condition checks if the character after the first non-zero digit is null, but it doesn't correctly handle cases where there's only one significant digit after leading zeros. Also, 'a[pos+1]' may be out of bounds if 'pos+1 >= len'.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This condition checks if the character after the first non-zero digit is null, but it doesn't correctly handle cases where there's only one significant digit after leading zeros. Also, 'a[pos+1]' may be out of bounds if 'pos+1 >= len'.]

45.reason: [This prints the entire input string followed by 'e0', which is incorrect because scientific notation requires normalization (only one non-zero digit before the decimal point). For example, input "5.3" would output "5.3e0", but should be "5.3e0" only if normalized properly 鈥 however, per problem statement, base must have one digit before decimal unless it's an integer with one digit. But more critically, this branch assumes s==1 means single digit before '.', but doesn't remove extra digits after '.']

For example, input "5.3" would output "5.3e0", but should be "5.3e0" only if normalized properly 鈥 however, per problem statement, base must have one digit before decimal unless it's an integer with one digit. But more critically, this branch assumes s==1 means single digit before '.', but doesn't remove extra digits after '.']
